differentials.name: elevated amniotic α-fetoprotein level OB-US (assessment difficult prior to 20 weeks GA as ventricles ordinarily constitute a large portion of cranial vault):
differentials.raw: elevated amniotic α-fetoprotein level OB-US (assessment difficult prior to 20 weeks GA as ventricles ordinarily constitute a large portion of cranial vault):

context: =  excess of CSF ← increased intraventricular pressure ← imbalance of CSF formation and absorption Pathophysiology: Signs of raised intracranial pressure (skull film): (a)  young infant / newborn √  increase in craniofacial ratio √  bulging of anterior fontanel √  sutural diastasis √  macrocephaly + frontal bossing √  "beaten brass" = "hammered silver" appearance = prominent digital impressions (wide range of normals in 4-10 years of age) (b)  adolescent / adult → changes in sella turcica: √  atrophy of anterior wall of dorsum sellae √  shortening of the dorsum sellae producing pointed appeara
